mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations during early-cycle phases, or autocorrelation dominates the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently coincide with positive 60-day equity returns, or the signal reverses direction when core inflation remains subdued.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of cases, or the unemployment rate fails to decline despite payroll growth, breaking the interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or quarterly data frequency causes persistent look-ahead bias.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3% signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes consistently precede positive 20-day equity returns, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.
